raw_response: I agree with Option C. Maintaining the same depth for the pulpal floor between a class 2 inlay and amalgam preparation helps ensure consistent retention forms and preserves as much healthy tooth structure as possible. Deviating too deeply could compromise structural integrity unnecessarily. Therefore, my final answer is still C. Maintaining consistency regarding the pulpal floor depth enhances predictability and minimizes risks associated with overly aggressive cavities preparations. Answer choice: C. Consistency in pulpal floor placement supports optimal clinical outcomes without excessive removal of sound tissue.
